sections.paragraph:
only in fut. יֵקַע i.q. נָקַע to be torn off, to be torn away; hence-

(1) to be dislocated, as a limb, Gen 32:26.

(2) metaph. to be alienated from any one, Jer 6:8 Eze 23:17, 18 Eze 23:18followed by מִן and מֵעַל.

Hiphil הֹוקִיעַ to hang upon a stake, to fix to a stake, a punishment by which the limbs were dislocated: [Perhaps simply to hang, in which the neck is dislocated], Num 25:4 2Sa 21:6, 2Sa 21:9.

Hophal pass. 2Sa 21:13.
